payload.message:
Because the market page had a state bug.

When you click a market, `useMarket()` starts fetching `/api/markets/:id`. During that fetch, `market` is temporarily `undefined`. The page was treating that same `undefined` state as “not found”, so you saw a false error first, then the real market appeared a few seconds later when the request finished.

I fixed that in [src/app/market/[id]/market-client.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/market/[id]/market-client.tsx): it now shows a loading skeleton while the request is in flight, and only shows `Market not found` after there is an actual error/empty result. I also added preloading for market detail data in [src/lib/use-data.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/lib/use-data.ts), [src/components/market-card.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/components/market-card.tsx), and [src/app/page.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/page.tsx) so hover/touch/quick-bet warms the market request before navigation.

`npm run build` passes locally with this fix. If you want, I can deploy this patch to Vercel now.

content.text:
Because the market page had a state bug.

When you click a market, `useMarket()` starts fetching `/api/markets/:id`. During that fetch, `market` is temporarily `undefined`. The page was treating that same `undefined` state as “not found”, so you saw a false error first, then the real market appeared a few seconds later when the request finished.

I fixed that in [src/app/market/[id]/market-client.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/market/[id]/market-client.tsx): it now shows a loading skeleton while the request is in flight, and only shows `Market not found` after there is an actual error/empty result. I also added preloading for market detail data in [src/lib/use-data.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/lib/use-data.ts), [src/components/market-card.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/components/market-card.tsx), and [src/app/page.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/page.tsx) so hover/touch/quick-bet warms the market request before navigation.

`npm run build` passes locally with this fix. If you want, I can deploy this patch to Vercel now.

payload.last_agent_message:
Because the market page had a state bug.

When you click a market, `useMarket()` starts fetching `/api/markets/:id`. During that fetch, `market` is temporarily `undefined`. The page was treating that same `undefined` state as “not found”, so you saw a false error first, then the real market appeared a few seconds later when the request finished.

I fixed that in [src/app/market/[id]/market-client.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/market/[id]/market-client.tsx): it now shows a loading skeleton while the request is in flight, and only shows `Market not found` after there is an actual error/empty result. I also added preloading for market detail data in [src/lib/use-data.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/lib/use-data.ts), [src/components/market-card.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/components/market-card.tsx), and [src/app/page.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/page.tsx) so hover/touch/quick-bet warms the market request before navigation.

`npm run build` passes locally with this fix. If you want, I can deploy this patch to Vercel now.

payload.message:
Deployed.

The market-page fix is now live on production:
`https://12polymarket.vercel.app`

This deploy includes:
- loading state instead of false `Market not found`
- market detail preloading on hover/touch
- preloading before quick-bet navigation

The deployment URL is `https://12polymarket-cs7jr73ne-kkem2684-7930s-projects.vercel.app`, and `12polymarket.vercel.app` now points to it.

payload.message: Now i have the prod domain which setting in vercel as 12polymarket.vercel.app but it didn't load the market from databasre

I’m checking the live production API and the Vercel project env configuration. If `12polymarket.vercel.app` loads but shows no market data, the likely cause is the production runtime using an empty or wrong `DATABASE_URL`, not the frontend.

payload.message:
It is loading from the database right now.

I checked the live production API on `https://12polymarket.vercel.app/api/markets` and it returned a real market row from Postgres, not empty mock data:

- title: `พี่โดมจะพูดคำว่า "สูญเสียตัวตน" ตอนประชุมตอนเย็น`
- creator: `Burapat`
- volume: `2000`
- createdAt: `2026-04-25T08:05:23.749Z`

So the production domain is hitting the DB.

What I also found is important: your Vercel build logs show this line:

- `Environment variables loaded from .env`

That means the deploy is still receiving the local `.env` file during `vercel deploy`, which is a bad setup. It can make builds inconsistent with the env vars configured in the Vercel dashboard.

So the current state is:

- runtime production API is returning DB data
- but builds are still polluted by local `.env`
- that needs to be cleaned up

Next fix I recommend:
1. Add a `.vercelignore` to exclude `.env*`
2. Keep `DATABASE_URL` only in Vercel project envs
3. Redeploy after that

If you want, I can make that cleanup and redeploy now.

So the production domain is hitting the DB.

What I also found is important: your Vercel build logs show this line:

That means the deploy is still receiving the local `.env` file during `vercel deploy`, which is a bad setup. It can make builds inconsistent with the env vars configured in the Vercel dashboard.

Next fix I recommend:

If you want, I can make that cleanup and redeploy now.

`https://12polymarket.vercel.app` is now on the new deployment:
`https://12polymarket-21ziqqxq7-kkem2684-7930s-projects.vercel.app`

I verified the important part in the new build logs:

- the old bad line is gone: `Environment variables loaded from .env`
- so the build is no longer actually loading env values from local `.env`
- Vercel still prints a warning: `Detected .env file, it is strongly recommended to use Vercel's env handling instead`

That warning means the file still exists in the repo/workspace, but it is not being used to load build env anymore. The runtime should now rely on the Vercel project envs.

So the current state is:
- production redeployed
- `12polymarket.vercel.app` updated
- build no longer loads `.env`
- runtime DB access should come from Vercel `DATABASE_URL`

If you want, I can do one more cleanup pass and remove the warning entirely by restructuring local env usage so Vercel never even detects a root `.env` file.

I added active-market preloading.

In [src/components/market-card.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/components/market-card.tsx), every active market card now prefetches:
- the `/market/[id]` route
- the `/api/markets/:id` data
- auth state needed on the bet page

content.text:
I added active-market preloading.

In [src/components/market-card.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/components/market-card.tsx), every active market card now prefetches:
- the `/market/[id]` route
- the `/api/markets/:id` data
- auth state needed on the bet page

That happens automatically for active cards, plus it still warms again on hover/focus/touch. I also added [src/app/market/[id]/loading.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/market/[id]/loading.tsx) so if a request still isn’t fully warm, navigation shows an instant skeleton instead of feeling blocked.

Validation passed with targeted lint and `npm run build`.

If you want this on production too, I can deploy it now.

payload.message:
Deployed.

The active-market preloading change is live on:
`https://12polymarket.vercel.app`

This deployment is:
`https://12polymarket-cocentsgz-kkem2684-7930s-projects.vercel.app`

It now:
- prefetches each active market route
- preloads each active market’s detail API
- keeps the market page loading shell as fallback

If market navigation still feels slow after this, the next issue is not missing preload anymore. It will be network latency or the live `/api/markets/:id` response time.

content.text: Let start and optimize the website 1. make the preload for leaderboard page 2. Now the prod in vercel didn't use the real database data so make it use (clean the market database first)
also do the optimization like caching, ... to the website

I changed the app so production behaves like a real shared environment instead of a demo build.

The leaderboard route now has an actual streaming loading shell in [src/app/leaderboard/loading.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/leaderboard/loading.tsx), and the navbar explicitly warms `/leaderboard` from [src/components/navbar.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/components/navbar.tsx). I also tightened client fetch reuse in [src/lib/use-data.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/lib/use-data.ts) and aligned API cache headers in [next.config.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/next.config.ts), [src/app/api/markets/route.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/api/markets/route.ts), [src/app/api/markets/[id]/route.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/api/markets/[id]/route.ts), and [src/app/api/leaderboard/route.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/api/leaderboard/route.ts).

For the database issue, [src/lib/prisma.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/lib/prisma.ts) now fails fast if `DATABASE_URL` is missing, and sample seeding is blocked in production in [src/app/api/seed/route.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/api/seed/route.ts) and hidden from the homepage in [src/app/page.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/page.tsx). I also removed the bad local `.env.production` override that was masking the real DB locally.

The live Postgres cleanup is done: `Market=0`, `Bet=0`, `PriceHistory=0`, and user `heldCoins` were reset to `0` so balances aren’t left inconsistent. Full validation passed with `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

Before the next Vercel deploy, make sure the project-level `DATABASE_URL` in Vercel is the real Supabase/Postgres URL you want to use. The app will now surface that misconfiguration immediately instead of silently drifting.
it still slow loading when click to leaderboard

The slow click was coming from the leaderboard page itself: it was a dynamic server render, so every navigation waited on fresh server work and DB reads.

I changed it to a client page in [src/app/leaderboard/page-client.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/leaderboard/page-client.tsx) and left [src/app/leaderboard/page.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/leaderboard/page.tsx) as a static wrapper. The page now uses the same SWR cache as the sidebar, so if leaderboard data was already fetched, the click renders immediately from warm client state. I also added explicit SWR preloading in [src/lib/use-data.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/lib/use-data.ts) and trigger it from the navbar link in [src/components/navbar.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/components/navbar.tsx) on mount, hover, focus, and touch.

Build passes again with `npm run build`, and `/leaderboard` is now static at the route level instead of dynamic. That should remove the slow transition you were feeling on click. If it still feels slow after deploy, the next bottleneck is the `/api/leaderboard` response itself, and I’d optimize that query path directly.
is it on prod

No. I changed the code locally and verified it builds, but I did not deploy it to Vercel from here.

If you want it on production, the next step is to deploy the current branch/repo to Vercel. After that, test `/leaderboard` on the live URL, because the improvement only shows up in prod after a new deployment.
deploy it to vercel

Deployed to Vercel production.

The new production deployment is `https://12polymarket-bjucqg544-kkem2684-7930s-projects.vercel.app` and it has been aliased to the stable prod URL: `https://12polymarket-kkem2684-7930s-projects.vercel.app`.

Check `/leaderboard` on the aliased prod URL now. If it still feels slow there, the remaining issue is no longer deployment state. It will be either live network latency or the `/api/leaderboard` response time in production.
it took 5 seccond to load the leaderboard page
Why it not also push to the domain https://12polymarket.vercel.app/

Because `12polymarket.vercel.app` was still manually aliased to an older deployment. The `--prod` deploy updated the project’s normal production aliases, but that specific plain `vercel.app` alias did not move with it.

I fixed it. `https://12polymarket.vercel.app/` now points to the latest deployment:
`https://12polymarket-bjucqg544-kkem2684-7930s-projects.vercel.app`

If you refresh `12polymarket.vercel.app` now, you should be on the new build.
why some time i click to the bet but it show no market found and then if wait for some seccond it can access the bet market page

Because the market page had a state bug.

When you click a market, `useMarket()` starts fetching `/api/markets/:id`. During that fetch, `market` is temporarily `undefined`. The page was treating that same `undefined` state as “not found”, so you saw a false error first, then the real market appeared a few seconds later when the request finished.

I fixed that in [src/app/market/[id]/market-client.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/market/[id]/market-client.tsx): it now shows a loading skeleton while the request is in flight, and only shows `Market not found` after there is an actual error/empty result. I also added preloading for market detail data in [src/lib/use-data.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/lib/use-data.ts), [src/components/market-card.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/components/market-card.tsx), and [src/app/page.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/page.tsx) so hover/touch/quick-bet warms the market request before navigation.

`npm run build` passes locally with this fix. If you want, I can deploy this patch to Vercel now.
make it to prod

Deployed.

The market-page fix is now live on production:
`https://12polymarket.vercel.app`

This deploy includes:
- loading state instead of false `Market not found`
- market detail preloading on hover/touch
- preloading before quick-bet navigation

The deployment URL is `https://12polymarket-cs7jr73ne-kkem2684-7930s-projects.vercel.app`, and `12polymarket.vercel.app` now points to it.
Now i have the prod domain which setting in vercel as 12polymarket.vercel.app but it didn't load the market from databasre

It is loading from the database right now.

I checked the live production API on `https://12polymarket.vercel.app/api/markets` and it returned a real market row from Postgres, not empty mock data:

- title: `พี่โดมจะพูดคำว่า "สูญเสียตัวตน" ตอนประชุมตอนเย็น`
- creator: `Burapat`
- volume: `2000`
- createdAt: `2026-04-25T08:05:23.749Z`

So the production domain is hitting the DB.

What I also found is important: your Vercel build logs show this line:

- `Environment variables loaded from .env`

That means the deploy is still receiving the local `.env` file during `vercel deploy`, which is a bad setup. It can make builds inconsistent with the env vars configured in the Vercel dashboard.

So the current state is:

- runtime production API is returning DB data
- but builds are still polluted by local `.env`
- that needs to be cleaned up

Next fix I recommend:
1. Add a `.vercelignore` to exclude `.env*`
2. Keep `DATABASE_URL` only in Vercel project envs
3. Redeploy after that

If you want, I can make that cleanup and redeploy now.
yes

Done.

`https://12polymarket.vercel.app` is now on the new deployment:
`https://12polymarket-21ziqqxq7-kkem2684-7930s-projects.vercel.app`

I verified the important part in the new build logs:

- the old bad line is gone: `Environment variables loaded from .env`
- so the build is no longer actually loading env values from local `.env`
- Vercel still prints a warning: `Detected .env file, it is strongly recommended to use Vercel's env handling instead`

That warning means the file still exists in the repo/workspace, but it is not being used to load build env anymore. The runtime should now rely on the Vercel project envs.

So the current state is:
- production redeployed
- `12polymarket.vercel.app` updated
- build no longer loads `.env`
- runtime DB access should come from Vercel `DATABASE_URL`

If you want, I can do one more cleanup pass and remove the warning entirely by restructuring local env usage so Vercel never even detects a root `.env` file.
make it do some preload on each market (active) cause it kinda slow when click to each bet

I added active-market preloading.

In [src/components/market-card.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/components/market-card.tsx), every active market card now prefetches:
- the `/market/[id]` route
- the `/api/markets/:id` data
- auth state needed on the bet page

That happens automatically for active cards, plus it still warms again on hover/focus/touch. I also added [src/app/market/[id]/loading.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/market/[id]/loading.tsx) so if a request still isn’t fully warm, navigation shows an instant skeleton instead of feeling blocked.

Validation passed with targeted lint and `npm run build`.

If you want this on production too, I can deploy it now.
yes deploy it

Deployed.

The active-market preloading change is live on:
`https://12polymarket.vercel.app`

This deployment is:
`https://12polymarket-cocentsgz-kkem2684-7930s-projects.vercel.app`

It now:
- prefetches each active market route
- preloads each active market’s detail API
- keeps the market page loading shell as fallback

If market navigation still feels slow after this, the next issue is not missing preload anymore. It will be network latency or the live `/api/markets/:id` response time.
for metagraph make it show detail about beting price, %, ...

I updated the market metadata/OG output.

Now shared market links include richer betting detail:
- `YES/NO` percentages
- implied price per share like `$0.60`
- total volume
- bet count

The changes are in:
- [src/lib/market-share.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/lib/market-share.ts)
- [src/app/api/og/market/[id]/route.tsx](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/app/api/og/market/[id]/route.tsx)

`npm run build` passes.

If you want this live on production, I can deploy it now.

# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.aggregated_output:
SciusPolyMarket is a private Polymarket-style prediction market app for friend groups. It uses Next.js, Prisma, and a Supabase Postgres database.

## Getting Started

Create `.env` from `.env.example`, then set `DATABASE_URL` to your Supabase Postgres URI and set `AUTH_SECRET`.

Push the schema and run the app:

```bash
npx prisma db push
npm run dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) with your browser.

Useful checks:

```bash
npm run lint
npm run build
node --experimental-strip-types src/lib/pricing.test.ts
```

## Features

- Username/password sign up and sign in with HTTP-only sessions.
- Supabase Postgres database through Prisma.
- LMSR probability pricing for YES/NO markets.
- Share metadata for market links using Open Graph/Twitter cards.
- Responsive Polymarket-style market list, market detail, chart, and betting UI.

{
  "name": "my-app",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev --webpack",
    "build": "next build",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "eslint",
    "test:pricing": "node --experimental-strip-types src/lib/pricing.test.ts",
    "postinstall": "prisma generate"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@base-ui/react": "^1.4.1",
    "@prisma/client": "^5.22.0",
    "class-variance-authority": "^0.7.1",
    "clsx": "^2.1.1",
    "lucide-react": "^1.8.0",
    "next": "16.2.4",
    "pg": "^8.20.0",
    "prisma": "^5.22.0",

    "react-dom": "19.2.4",
    "recharts": "^3.8.1",
    "shadcn": "^4.4.0",
    "swr": "^2.4.1",
    "tailwind-merge": "^3.5.0",
    "tw-animate-css": "^1.4.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": "^4",
    "@types/node": "^20",
    "@types/react": "^19",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19",
    "eslint": "^9",
    "eslint-config-next": "16.2.4",
    "tailwindcss": "^4",
    "typescript": "^5",
    "vercel": "^52.0.0"
  }
}

SciusPolyMarket is a private Polymarket-style prediction market app for friend groups. It uses Next.js, Prisma, and a Supabase Postgres database.

## Getting Started

Create `.env` from `.env.example`, then set `DATABASE_URL` to your Supabase Postgres URI and set `AUTH_SECRET`.

Push the schema and run the app:

```bash
npx prisma db push
npm run dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) with your browser.

Useful checks:

```bash
npm run lint
npm run build
node --experimental-strip-types src/lib/pricing.test.ts
```

## Features

- Username/password sign up and sign in with HTTP-only sessions.
- Supabase Postgres database through Prisma.
- LMSR probability pricing for YES/NO markets.
- Share metadata for market links using Open Graph/Twitter cards.
- Responsive Polymarket-style market list, market detail, chart, and betting UI.

# Supabase Postgres connection string.
# Supabase dashboard -> Project Settings -> Database -> Connection string -> URI.
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://postgres:<password>@db.<project-ref>.supabase.co:5432/postgres?sslmode=require"

# Used to sign HTTP-only auth sessions. Use a long random value in production.
AUTH_SECRET="replace-with-a-long-random-secret"

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

model User {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  name      String   @unique
  passwordHash String?
  coins     Float    @default(10000)
  heldCoins Float    @default(0)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
}

model Market {
  id             String         @id @default(uuid())
  title          String
  description    String
  category       String
  imageUrl       String?
  endDate        DateTime
  resolved       Boolean        @default(false)
  outcome        String?
  multiplier     Float          @default(2.0)
  liquidity      Float          @default(5000)
  optionYesLabel String         @default("Yes")
  optionNoLabel  String         @default("No")
  creatorName    String
  createdAt      DateTime       @default(now())
  updatedAt      DateTime       @updatedAt
  bets           Bet[]
  priceHistory   PriceHistory[]

  @@index([resolved])
  @@index([createdAt])
  @@index([category])
  @@index([creatorName])
}

model Bet {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  marketId  String
  market    Market   @relation(fields: [marketId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  userName  String
  coins     Float
  choice    String
  price     Float
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([marketId])
  @@index([userName])
  @@index([marketId, choice])
  @@index([createdAt])
}

model PriceHistory {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  marketId  String
  market    Market   @relation(fields: [marketId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  yesPrice  Float
  noPrice   Float
  timestamp DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([marketId])
  @@index([marketId, timestamp])
}

+- Client-side preloading for leaderboard and active market pages

+

+- `GET /api/leaderboard`
+- `POST /api/auth/sign-up`
+- `POST /api/auth/sign-in`
+- `POST /api/auth/sign-out`
+- `GET /api/auth/me`
+- `POST /api/seed`
+- `GET /api/og/market/[id]`

# Twelve Polymarket

Twelve Polymarket is a private prediction market app for friend groups. It is built with Next.js 16, React 19, Prisma, and Postgres, and uses LMSR-style pricing for YES/NO markets.

## Stack

- Next.js `16.2.4`
- React `19.2.4`
- Prisma `5`
- Postgres via `DATABASE_URL`
- SWR for client data fetching and preloading
- Tailwind CSS 4 + shadcn/ui primitives
- Vercel for production deploys

## Core Features

- Username/password auth with signed HTTP-only cookies
- Market creation, market detail, and bet placement
- LMSR pricing with YES/NO share prices and price history
- Leaderboard based on user balances
- Open Graph market images with price, %, volume, and bet count
- Client-side preloading for leaderboard and active market pages
- API cache headers tuned for Vercel edge caching

## Environment Variables

Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and fill in the values:

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Required:

- `DATABASE_URL`: Postgres connection string
- `AUTH_SECRET`: secret used to sign auth sessions

Notes:

- Production must use the Vercel project environment variables, not local `.env` values.
- `.vercelignore` excludes `.env*` from deploy uploads so builds do not accidentally use local secrets.
- `src/lib/prisma.ts` fails fast if `DATABASE_URL` is missing.

## Local Development

Install dependencies:

```bash
npm install
```

Generate Prisma client and sync schema:

```bash
npx prisma generate
npx prisma db push
```

Start the app:

```bash
npm run dev
```

Open `http://localhost:3000`.

## Useful Commands

```bash
npm run dev
npm run lint
npm run build
npm run test:pricing
```

## Database Model

Main Prisma models:

- `User`: balance, held balance, and auth hash
- `Market`: prediction market metadata and resolution state
- `Bet`: individual YES/NO wagers with stored entry price
- `PriceHistory`: time series for YES/NO price movement

Schema lives in [prisma/schema.prisma](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/prisma/schema.prisma).

## API Overview

Main routes:

- `GET/POST /api/markets`
- `GET/PATCH /api/markets/[id]`
- `POST /api/bets`
- `GET /api/leaderboard`
- `POST /api/auth/sign-up`
- `POST /api/auth/sign-in`
- `POST /api/auth/sign-out`
- `GET /api/auth/me`
- `POST /api/seed`
- `GET /api/og/market/[id]`

Important behavior:

- `/api/seed` is disabled in production.
- `/api/auth/me` is private and `no-store`.
- leaderboard and market APIs send cache headers for Vercel caching.

## Performance Notes

The app already includes a few route-level optimizations:

- `/leaderboard` has a loading shell and client-side SWR preload
- active market cards preload route code and market detail data
- market detail avoids false "not found" UI while data is loading
- leaderboard data and market detail data are deduped in SWR

The main preload logic lives in [src/lib/use-data.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/lib/use-data.ts).

## Production Deploy

Deploy to Vercel:

```bash
npx vercel deploy --prod --yes
```

Current production domain:

- `https://12polymarket.vercel.app`

Deployment rules:

- set `DATABASE_URL` and `AUTH_SECRET` in the Vercel project settings
- do not rely on `.env` files during deploy
- if production data looks wrong, verify the Vercel project envs first

## Project Structure

```text
src/app/                  App Router pages and API routes
src/components/           UI and feature components
src/lib/                  Auth, Prisma, pricing, caching, and shared helpers
prisma/                   Prisma schema and migrations
scripts/                  Small maintenance scripts
oldcode/                  Archived older implementation
```

- Tailwind CSS 4 + shadcn/ui primitives
- Vercel for production deploys

## Core Features

- Username/password auth with signed HTTP-only cookies
- Market creation, market detail, and bet placement
- LMSR pricing with YES/NO share prices and price history
- Leaderboard based on user balances
- Open Graph market images with price, %, volume, and bet count
- Client-side preloading for leaderboard and active market pages
- API cache headers tuned for Vercel edge caching

## Environment Variables

Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and fill in the values:

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Required:

- `DATABASE_URL`: Postgres connection string
- `AUTH_SECRET`: secret used to sign auth sessions

Notes:

- Production must use the Vercel project environment variables, not local `.env` values.
- `.vercelignore` excludes `.env*` from deploy uploads so builds do not accidentally use local secrets.
- `src/lib/prisma.ts` fails fast if `DATABASE_URL` is missing.

## Local Development

Install dependencies:

```bash
npm install
```

Generate Prisma client and sync schema:

```bash
npx prisma generate
npx prisma db push
```

Start the app:

```bash
npm run dev
```

Open `http://localhost:3000`.

## Useful Commands

```bash
npm run dev
npm run lint
npm run build
npm run test:pricing
```

## Database Model

Main Prisma models:

- `User`: balance, held balance, and auth hash
- `Market`: prediction market metadata and resolution state
- `Bet`: individual YES/NO wagers with stored entry price
- `PriceHistory`: time series for YES/NO price movement

Schema lives in [prisma/schema.prisma](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/prisma/schema.prisma).

## API Overview

Main routes:

- `GET/POST /api/markets`
- `GET/PATCH /api/markets/[id]`
- `POST /api/bets`
- `GET /api/leaderboard`
- `POST /api/auth/sign-up`
- `POST /api/auth/sign-in`
- `POST /api/auth/sign-out`
- `GET /api/auth/me`
- `POST /api/seed`
- `GET /api/og/market/[id]`

Important behavior:

- `/api/seed` is disabled in production.
- `/api/auth/me` is private and `no-store`.
- leaderboard and market APIs send cache headers for Vercel caching.

## Performance Notes

The app already includes a few route-level optimizations:

- `/leaderboard` has a loading shell and client-side SWR preload
- active market cards preload route code and market detail data
- market detail avoids false "not found" UI while data is loading
- leaderboard data and market detail data are deduped in SWR

The main preload logic lives in [src/lib/use-data.ts](/home/burapat/Work/Twelve-Polymarket/src/lib/use-data.ts).

## Production Deploy

Deploy to Vercel:

```bash
npx vercel deploy --prod --yes
```

Current production domain:

- `https://12polymarket.vercel.app`

Deployment rules:

- set `DATABASE_URL` and `AUTH_SECRET` in the Vercel project settings
- do not rely on `.env` files during deploy
- if production data looks wrong, verify the Vercel project envs first

## Project Structure

```text
src/app/                  App Router pages and API routes
src/components/           UI and feature components
src/lib/                  Auth, Prisma, pricing, caching, and shared helpers
prisma/                   Prisma schema and migrations
scripts/                  Small maintenance scripts
oldcode/                  Archived older implementation
```

# Project Notes

## Stack

- Next.js `16.2.4` App Router
- React `19.2.4`
- Prisma with Postgres
- SWR on the client for market, auth, and leaderboard data
- Vercel for production deploys

## Environment Rules

- `DATABASE_URL` is required. `src/lib/prisma.ts` is expected to fail fast if it is missing.
- `AUTH_SECRET` signs the app's HTTP-only session cookies.
- Production must use Vercel environment variables.
- Do not make deploys depend on local `.env` files.
- Keep `.vercelignore` excluding `.env*` unless there is a very specific reason to change that.

## Product Rules

- This is a private friend-group prediction market, not a public Polymarket clone.
- Markets are YES/NO and priced with LMSR logic in `src/lib/pricing.ts`.
- `coins` is liquid balance; `heldCoins` is committed balance on unresolved bets.
- Seed data is for local/dev only. `POST /api/seed` must remain blocked in production.
- Production should use the real database, not demo fallback data.

## Existing Performance Work

- `/leaderboard` uses a client page wrapper, loading shell, and SWR preloading.
- Active market cards preload market detail data and route code.
- Market pages should show loading UI while fetching, not a false "not found" state.
- API routes for markets and leaderboard already set cache headers for Vercel.

When changing navigation or data loading, preserve or improve these behaviors rather than reverting to slower server-only navigation.

## Important Files

- `src/lib/use-data.ts`: SWR hooks, fetcher, preload helpers
- `src/lib/prisma.ts`: Prisma client and env enforcement
- `src/lib/auth.ts`: session cookie auth
- `src/lib/pricing.ts`: LMSR pricing logic
- `src/lib/market-share.ts`: derived market percentages and metadata text
- `src/app/api/markets/route.ts`: market list API
- `src/app/api/markets/[id]/route.ts`: market detail and resolution API
- `src/app/api/bets/route.ts`: bet placement
- `src/app/api/leaderboard/route.ts`: leaderboard API
- `src/app/api/og/market/[id]/route.tsx`: OG image generation

## Deployment Notes

- Standard production deploy command:
  - `npx vercel deploy --prod --yes`
- Main production domain:
  - `https://12polymarket.vercel.app`
- If the wrong deployment is serving, verify the alias on Vercel after deploy.

## Documentation Expectation

If you change runtime behavior, update `README.md` when the setup, deployment flow, API behavior, or operational rules change.
